task.objective: Classify text as human-written or identify which specific AI model generated it

evaluation.note: Models must perform well across ALL categories, not just the most frequent ones.

train.csv.description: Training dataset with text samples and labels

test.csv.description: Test dataset with text samples, no labels. Has unnamed index column + TEXT.

dataset.class_imbalance_note: Heavily imbalanced — Human (63.3%) dominates; DeepSeek and Claude have only 80 samples each (3.3%)

forbidden: Pre-trained AI-generated code detectors are FORBIDDEN
Additional data beyond training set is FORBIDDEN
allowed: Public code sharing on Kaggle forums/notebooks
rules.data_note: Human-written texts are taken from websites, NOT written by organizers

key_challenges: Severe class imbalance (Human: 1520 vs DeepSeek/Claude: 80 each)
Macro F1 requires good performance on ALL classes including minority ones
Distinguishing between different AI models is harder than human vs AI
Modern LLMs produce highly fluent, human-like text
Cannot use pre-trained AI detectors (e.g., GPTZero, OpenAI detector)
suggested_approaches: TF-IDF + traditional ML (SVM, XGBoost, LightGBM) as baseline
Class balancing: SMOTE, class weights, oversampling minority classes

project_status.best_model: Ensemble (DeBERTa-v3-base + SVC + LR) with stacking meta-learner
